$comment: Register semantics for the PumpSaver Plus IR broadcast (spec v0.3, reverse engineered on a 233P-1.5). confidence: verified = cross-checked against external ground truth, arithmetic structure, or live actuation (2026-07-09 field session: knob sweeps + a deliberately induced underload trip observed on the wire); candidate = consistent hypothesis (strong = rivals refuted); unknown = observed value only. Observed values are from a 233P-1.5 on a ~240V well pump.

protocol.word: 0x90 | reg:8 | value:16 BE, MSB-first, trailing zero-bits omitted
protocol.ring_block: 0x19-0x75, round-robined in 4 chunks, full refresh every ~5.8 s
protocol.mcu: Microchip PIC16F684 (bit-banged IR, no UART, 256B EEPROM holds ring+cal)

registers.name: pump_starts
registers.notes: User-clearable pump-start counter. +1 within ~2 s of every ground-truth pump start; monotone across 8 months of captures. Counter width beyond this 16-bit word unresolved (no clear or rollover captured).

registers.name: power
registers.notes: TRUE active power. ~29 W idle (device self-draw), ~820 W running, inrush peaks ~2250. Reads exactly 0 during a trip lockout (vs 29 idle-armed): the zero distinguishes lockout from idle.

registers.name: voltage
registers.notes: Live line voltage x10. Tracks sags from any load on the leg. Observed lifetime range 233.1-248.8 V.

registers.name: current
registers.notes: LEG-SUM channel: reads ~2x true motor current while the pump runs (both 240V hot legs pass the sensing path; true running amps = value/200); device idle draw is single-count (value/100).

registers.name: drywell_trip
registers.notes: Dry-well/underload trip point in watts = SENSITIVITY knob x calibration power. LIVE-VERIFIED: tracks the knob in real time (observed 744-885 W during a sweep; NORMAL = ~781 = 82% of 953; the + endstop measured ~885 = 93%, past the documented 90%). Raising it above running power tripped the relay in ~4 s. Pot dither ~±5 W. Formerly misread as power factor x1000 (781 was numerology).

registers.name: restart_delay_set
registers.notes: RESTART DELAY knob setting in minutes. LIVE-VERIFIED: tracks the knob in real time (swept 16-77 min). Dial silkscreen is poorly calibrated: dial 'just above 50' read 66-77, dial 'just under 2' read 16. Changing it mid-lockout re-bases the running countdown. Formerly misread as a 4.1 s trip delay.

registers.name: restart_remaining_hi
registers.notes: High word of the 32-bit restart-delay countdown {0x15,0x16}: remaining = (0x15<<16 | 0x16)/256 seconds. Zero except during a trip lockout. LIVE-VERIFIED: initialized to exactly the setting (2280.00 s = 38 min), decrements 1 s/s, re-bases if the knob moves mid-lockout, zeroes at RESET.

registers.name: restart_remaining_lo
registers.notes: Low word of the restart-delay countdown (see 0x15). Decrements by 256 per second during lockout; 0 otherwise.

registers.name: fault_amps
registers.notes: Current at the newest fault, latched at trip (same leg-sum scale as 0x12; read 880 = 8.80 leg-sum at a normal-flow induced trip). Was 0 for the historical code-4 fault (pump never ran = rapid-cycle signature).

registers.name: fault_volts
registers.notes: Voltage at the newest fault, latched at trip (jumped 2112 -> 2422 at the induced trip). The long-constant 2112 was the historical code-4 fault's voltage (211.2 V) - NOT a low-voltage trip setting as previously guessed.

registers.name: fault_watts
registers.notes: Power at the newest fault, latched at trip (read 836 = last reading before cutoff). 0 for the historical code-4 fault.

registers.name: run_minutes
registers.notes: User-clearable cumulative run-time in minutes; +1 per 60 s of running; tick carry-chains prove the wire encoding. Width beyond this word unresolved.

registers.name: calibration_power
registers.notes: Calibration power (953 W): the dry-well trip point (0x13) = SENSITIVITY% x this value, confirmed by the live knob sweep (781/953 = 82% at NORMAL).

registers.name: calibration_voltage
registers.notes: 238.4 V, within 2% of modal live V. Refuted as max-V record.

registers.name: overcurrent_trip
registers.notes: 1223 = exactly 125% x implied cal current (leg-sum scale). Refuted as min/max-amps record.

registers.name: const_0x07
registers.notes: 0xB004 for 8 months, then low byte dropped 4 -> 1 at the induced trip (45060 -> 45057), mirroring 0x75's high byte +3 at the next start. A paired bookkeeping mechanism (restart credits / rapid-cycle window?) - under analysis.

registers.name: const_0x08
registers.notes: = 0x03 (1223); duplication unexplained.

registers.name: oc_trip_delay
registers.notes: 50 = 5.0 s fixed overcurrent trip delay. Rivals refuted: the restart-delay setting proved to live in 0x14, and the model has no CT.

registers.name: high_voltage_trip_or_max
registers.notes: 248.8 V. High-voltage trip OR max-volts-since-cal (live V once grazed exactly 2488 for one sample).

registers.name: firmware_version
registers.notes: 1027 = 0x0403 = firmware v4.03, matching the SymCom family's packed-version convention exactly.

registers.name: const_0x0C
registers.notes: 10277 = 0x2825. Model-ID reading weakened (family convention stores literal decimal codes). Possibly a serial/date word.

registers.name: min_or_nominal_voltage
registers.notes: 231.5 V. Min-volts-since-cal or nominal voltage; refuted as max-V record.

blocks.notes: Observed constant 0. CT size (n/a on non-CT models) and max/min-amps records remain unlocated.

20 x 4-bit fault codes, packed MSB-first, NEWEST FIRST. Ring shift observed live: an induced underload trip pushed code 1 in front (0x4111 -> 0x1411). Code 1 = dry-well/underload (proven twice: retry arithmetic + live inducement). Enum hypothesis: 0=none, 2=overcurrent, 3=voltage, 4=rapid-cycle (the historical code-4's latched conditions - V=211.2, A=0, W=0, pump never running - fit rapid-cycle).

19 x (W, V x10, A x100) snapshots of faults #2..#20 (record k at 0x1E+3k, newest first). The NEWEST fault's conditions live in the 0x04/0x05/0x06 latches and are pushed into slot 0 when the NEXT fault occurs - observed live. Historical A-column scale (leg-sum vs single-count) under re-analysis now that the latch scale is known.
20 x 3-byte fault timestamps: 24-bit BE, run-clock MINUTES, newest first. Verified live: the induced trip's stamp read exactly the run-clock at trip (62,450 min).

Trailer word. Static 0x7E3C for 8 months, then high byte +3 (-> 0x813C) at the first pump start after the induced trip, mirroring 0x07's -3 at the trip. Paired bookkeeping - under analysis. (The old 'code-4 timestamp minus 256' reading was numerology.)
